source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An open circuit test is performed on a 50 Hz transformer, using a variable frequency source and keeping the V/f ratio constant, to separate its eddy current and hysteresis losses. The variation of core loss per frequency as a function of frequency is shown in the <image>. The hysteresis and eddy current losses of the transformer at 25 Hz respectively are ______.

multi_step: Solving appears to require reading a graph and then separating total core loss into hysteresis and eddy-current components
The question asks for two outputs ('hysteresis and eddy current losses ... at 25 Hz respectively'), indicating more than one derived quantity
The prompt includes the condition of keeping V/f constant, which must be combined with the graph information rather than used as a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with specific numeric values ('250 W and 62.5 W')
There are 4 multiple-choice options, enabling straightforward answer checking
The target quantities are numerical physics outputs that can be checked against graph reading and standard loss relations
